title: Implement centralized comment marker helpers
description:
## Goal
Add `src/comment-markers.js` to centralize hidden marker rendering and parsing for status comments, history comments, runner IDs, session data maps, and linked PR references.

## Required behavior
Export constants for the status/history marker names and helpers such as `renderRunnerIdMarker`, `parseRunnerId`, `renderSessionDataMarker`, `parseSessionData`, and `parseLinkedPrReference`. Parsing must tolerate empty bodies, missing markers, malformed JSON, older comments, and normal Markdown around the markers.

## Rationale
Follow-up runs currently depend on hidden comment state. Duplicated regexes make it easy for a comment copy edit to break continuity. Centralizing this logic makes state reconciliation and comment generation safer.

## Success criteria
Existing comments remain parseable. New render helpers produce the same marker strings currently expected by the action. No GitHub API calls are made in this module.
close_reason: Added centralized comment marker render/parse helpers, rewired call sites, and added tolerance tests

title: Document preflight-only and dry-run semantics
description:
## Goal
Update README, docs site, example workflow, and workflow template to explain preflight-only behavior if the input is accepted.

## Required documentation
Clarify that `dry-run=true` still starts a Netlify Agent but skips commits/PR creation, while `preflight-only=true` validates setup and does not start an agent. Include examples and troubleshooting guidance for failed checks.

## Rationale
The distinction is subtle and important. Documentation should prevent users from assuming dry-run is a no-external-call mode.

## Success criteria
Docs drift checker understands and validates the documented input and examples.
close_reason: Documented preflight-only vs dry-run semantics with examples and troubleshooting

title: Epic: Agent Runner hardening roadmap
description:
## Background
This epic tracks the reliability and operator-experience hardening plan for the Netlify Agent Runner GitHub Action. The selected scope is: deterministic scenario harness, state reconciliation, preflight validation, local simulator CLI, docs/template drift checking, canonical slug cleanup, richer failure taxonomy, GitHub Actions step summaries, and branch-based verification in the example repository.

## Canonical slug
All public consumer examples and branch verification should treat `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@v1` as the current release slug. Development validation should use this repository branch ref, e.g. `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@dw/actions-updates`, in `https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example` before any `@main` or release tag movement.

## Non-goals
Do not rewrite the whole action runner, replace Netlify CLI integration wholesale, or change existing defaults unexpectedly. Do not conflate `dry-run` with the proposed `preflight-only` mode.

## Success criteria
The resulting backlog is detailed enough that future agents can implement from beads without consulting the original markdown plan. The graph must remain acyclic and include unit, scenario, docs, and example-repo verification tasks.
close_reason: Planning context captured in detailed implementation beads; not an implementation task.

title: Add failure stage/category outputs from action orchestration
description:
## Goal
Teach the composite action flow to expose structured failure context where practical, such as `failure-stage`, `failure-category`, and sanitized `agent-error` values.

## Scope
Update the shell runner step in `action.yml` to emit stage/category hints at major failure points: environment validation, site lookup, agent create, follow-up session create, polling timeout, agent failure, commit to branch, and PR creation. Avoid a large rewrite of the shell flow in this task.

## Rationale
The error comment generator can classify raw messages, but explicit stage hints improve accuracy and make step summaries clearer.

## Success criteria
Existing outputs remain unchanged. New outputs improve diagnostics without breaking consumers who ignore them.
close_reason: Added structured runtime failure outputs and stage/category hints in action orchestration; kept existing outputs backward-compatible; updated wiring/docs expectations.

title: Add unit and scenario tests for state reconciliation
description:
## Goal
Add thorough tests for `state-reconciliation` and the refactored `extract-agent-id` behavior.

## Coverage requirements
Empty state returns `start-new-run`; valid status comment runner wins over PR body runner; PR body fallback works; malformed session JSON returns empty map plus warning; linked PR references are detected; agent run URL is reconstructed when site and runner are known; unknown marker formats do not throw.

## Scenario coverage
Include at least one fixture where an issue has a linked PR and must redirect users to the PR rather than starting a duplicate issue-based run.

## Success criteria
The tests demonstrate safe behavior for the main ways state can be missing, stale, or corrupted.
close_reason: Expanded unit and fixture scenario tests for reconciliation and extract behavior

title: Finalize preflight-only public contract
description:
## Goal
Decide and encode the public contract for setup validation before implementation spreads assumptions. The planning recommendation is a new optional input `preflight-only`, separate from existing `dry-run` semantics.

## Required decision points
Confirm whether `preflight-only` should be public; whether preflight failure should post a status comment; whether manual dispatch should summary-only or comment; how `dry-run=true` plus `preflight-only=true` behaves; and whether failure category should become a formal output.

## Rationale
`dry-run` currently still starts the agent and skips PR creation/commits. A no-agent validation path is valuable, but changing dry-run semantics would surprise existing users.

## Success criteria
Implementation tasks can proceed from a documented contract without ambiguity.
close_reason: Finalized preflight-only contract decisions with explicit policy doc and contract tests

title: Route error comments through failure taxonomy
description:
## Goal
Update `src/generate-error-comment.js` so failure comments use the failure taxonomy rather than a mostly generic message.

## Required behavior
Render category-specific title, summary, remediation bullets, retryability where useful, GitHub logs link, Netlify dashboard link if runner/site are available, and the hidden status marker. Preserve existing provider/model fallback hints for model unavailability.

## Rationale
Issue/PR comments are the visible user support surface. They should say what failed and what to do next without making users spelunk raw logs first.

## Success criteria
Failure comments are specific, actionable, and still safe for public issue/PR visibility. Error text must be truncated and sanitized before display.
close_reason: Routed error comment generation through failure taxonomy with sanitized excerpts and tests

title: Add scenario tests for major GitHub event paths
description:
## Goal
Add `src/scenario-harness.test.js` covering the main user workflows and failure branches.

## Coverage requirements
New issue trigger, PR follow-up comment, issue comment with linked PR redirect, workflow dispatch explicit model, bot sender skip, untrusted fork PR skip, malformed marker safe fallback, missing session data warning, model unavailable taxonomy mapping, timeout taxonomy mapping, and step summary output.

## Rationale
These are the workflows most likely to regress during hardening. They should be deterministic and network-free.

## Success criteria
`bun test src/*.test.js` includes scenario coverage with clear trace output on failure.
close_reason: Expanded scenario-harness tests across major event paths and taxonomy/summary mappings; focused scenario suite passes

title: Create deterministic fixture corpus for action scenarios
description:
## Goal
Create a compact fixture corpus for GitHub events, mocked GitHub API responses, and mocked Netlify CLI/API responses used by scenario tests and the simulator.

## Required fixture groups
Events: issue opened body trigger, issue opened title trigger, issue comment on issue, issue comment on PR, pull request target body trigger, pull request review comment, pull request review, workflow dispatch, untrusted fork PR, bot comment.

GitHub responses: collaborator admin/read, timeline with and without linked PR, existing status comment with runner, malformed session marker, PR body with runner marker.

Netlify responses: get site success/failure, agent create success/model unavailable, agent show running/completed/failed, sessions list success.

## Success criteria
Fixtures are minimal but realistic and documented enough for new scenarios to be added quickly.
close_reason: Added deterministic fixtures (events/github/netlify), corpus README, and validation tests with focused pass

title: Add simulator tests and maintainer docs
description:
## Goal
Add tests and documentation for the simulator CLI.

## Coverage requirements
Test valid fixture exit 0, missing fixture exit non-zero, `--format json` parseability, `--format markdown` content, and warning propagation from state reconciliation. Document common usage in README or a maintainer docs section.

## Rationale
The simulator should be easy for future maintainers and agents to use correctly.

## Success criteria
The CLI is discoverable through `package.json` script such as `simulate` and through docs.
close_reason: Added simulator tests and maintainer docs

title: Implement preflight validation module
description:
## Goal
Add `src/preflight.js` to validate setup and configuration before expensive agent work begins.

## Required checks
Static checks: auth token input present, site ID input present, valid default model, positive integer timeout, GitHub token present, trigger context extracted, issue number available when comments are needed. Runtime checks: Netlify CLI availability/install status where practical, Netlify `getSite` resolution, GitHub repo access, and comment permission where practical.

## Result shape
Return `ok`, `checks`, `warnings`, and `failures`, with failures compatible with the failure taxonomy.

## Success criteria
The module is testable without live calls by injecting check implementations or fixtures.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic preflight validation module with injectable runtime checks and tests

title: Run live example-repo smoke tests against branch ref
description:
## Goal
After implementation, run live smoke tests in `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example` using the branch ref from this repository, not the release tag or main ref.

## Required evidence
Record the action ref tested, workflow run URLs, issue/PR URLs, observed status comments, GitHub step summaries, and Netlify dashboard/deploy links where applicable. Verify that no main tag/ref was overwritten as part of testing.

## Scenarios
At minimum run preflight-only, dry-run, normal issue-created PR flow, PR follow-up flow, and one intentional failure such as invalid site ID or model unavailable fixture if safely reproducible.

## Success criteria
All expected behavior works from the branch ref in the example repo before any release or main tag update is considered.
notes:
Live verification evidence (2026-04-21):

Action/code under test:
- Initial workflow_dispatch branch verification used `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@dc8936170e8ecbd1704c95d49b5acfd756f5c08d` from example branch `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example@verify/dw-actions-updates-20260421-de26190`.
- Default-branch event verification temporarily pinned the example repo workflow to `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@7833f2689323a2584e73ca53b6cf1d22e92cb197`.
- Example default-branch pin commit: `33a14e4bed37f695d0fe2d7bd36a1aae2572ffc3`.
- Example default-branch rollback commit: `de8d8e2bad5357b50f61662b00bc76560ef4636f`.
- Stable ref check: example `master` was restored to `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@main`; action repo `main`/tags were not moved.

Prior blocker evidence:
- Run `24703482828`: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24703482828
- Old action SHA `de26190861428e7efa11b46c0a35d0781f22cd0f` failed during job setup loading `action.yml` line 1185 with an unclosed expression. This was fixed before the successful reruns.

Scenario S1 / preflight-only success:
- Run URL: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24703589945
- Job URL: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24703589945/job/72251887707
- Result: success.
- Evidence: job downloaded `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@dc8936170e8ecbd1704c95d49b5acfd756f5c08d`; preflight output reported `preflight-ok=true`; execution summary logged `Status: skipped`, `Preflight-only input: true`, `Preflight ok: true`, `Should continue: false`, and `Preflight passed. preflight-only=true, so the agent run will not start.`
- Comments: none expected for `workflow_dispatch`.

Scenario S3 / dry-run prompt:
- Run URL: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24703620844
- Job URL: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24703620844/job/72251980061
- Result: success.
- Evidence: job downloaded pinned action SHA; resolved site `agent-runner-github-action-example`; created Netlify Agent runner `69e6f951c2e560c3e0709573`; agent reached `done`; log explicitly said `Preview mode — skipping PR creation and commits`; execution summary logged `Status: success`, `Dry-run: true`, `Preflight ok: true`, `Should continue: true`.
- Netlify dashboard URL: https://app.netlify.com/projects/agent-runner-github-action-example/agent-runs/69e6f951c2e560c3e0709573
- Deploy URL: none emitted in action outputs/log.
- PR URL: none emitted, expected for dry-run.

Scenario S6 / intentional invalid-site failure:
- Run URL: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24703681801
- Job URL: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24703681801/job/72252159142
- Result: failure, as expected for invalid site.
- Evidence: dispatch used `site_id_override=invalid-site-id`; `getSite` emitted `JSONHTTPError: Not Found`; site resolved as `unknown`; agent creation failed with `Error: The project can't be found`; step summary env had `FAILURE_CATEGORY=agent-create-failed`, `FAILURE_STAGE=create-agent`, and `AGENT_ERROR=Error: The project can't be found`; execution summary logged `Status: failure`, `Preflight ok: true`, `Should continue: true`.

Scenario S4 / normal issue-created PR flow:
- Issue: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/issues/85
- Run URL: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24704676725
- Job URL: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24704676725/job/72255134681
- Result: success.
- Evidence: event was `issues`; example workflow head SHA was `33a14e4bed37f695d0fe2d7bd36a1aae2572ffc3`; job downloaded `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@7833f2689323a2584e73ca53b6cf1d22e92cb197`; trigger check was true; context was `Issue #85`; model was `codex`; dry-run was false; preflight ok true; should continue true.
- Agent runner: `69e70278c2e560dcef709486`; dashboard URL: https://app.netlify.com/projects/agent-runner-github-action-example/agent-runs/69e70278c2e560dcef709486
- Created PR: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/pull/86
- Issue redirect comment: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/issues/85#issuecomment-4285997393
- PR status comment: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/pull/86#issuecomment-4286002609
- PR history comment: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/pull/86#issuecomment-4286002643
- PR commit from first run: `400081ffc38c0f9e02dea51c46af6ac9a988fee4`.
- Deploy preview: https://deploy-preview-86--agent-runner-github-action-example.netlify.app
- Deploy log: https://app.netlify.com/projects/agent-runner-github-action-example/deploys/69e702bb30474c0008bb0de7

Scenario S5 / PR follow-up flow:
- Follow-up comment: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/pull/86#issuecomment-4286004986
- Run URL: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24704748749
- Job URL: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24704748749/job/72255365523
- Result: success.
- Evidence: event was `issue_comment`; job downloaded `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@7833f2689323a2584e73ca53b6cf1d22e92cb197`; trigger check was true; context was `PR #86`; model was `codex`; dry-run was false.
- Existing runner id was retained: `69e70278c2e560dcef709486`.
- Checkout started from prior PR commit `400081ffc38c0f9e02dea51c46af6ac9a988fee4`, proving continuity on the PR branch.
- Follow-up commit: `d484bafcde20d5b837b95a3a06a15b119c2e8d47`.
- Existing status comment was updated in place: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/pull/86#issuecomment-4286002609
- Existing history comment was updated in place: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/pull/86#issuecomment-4286002643
- History now shows Run 2 and Run 1, and the session data map contains both session ids with run URLs and commit SHAs.
- Bot-comment guardrail runs skipped as expected: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24704791996 and https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24704793918
- One `pull_request_target` run was cancelled by normal concurrency/sync after the PR branch update: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24704790682

Rollback:
- Reverted example `master` workflow from the temporary SHA pin back to `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@main` in `de8d8e2bad5357b50f61662b00bc76560ef4636f`.
- Remote example workflow was verified after rollback.
close_reason: Completed live example-repo verification against pinned branch SHA

title: Add unit tests for comment marker helpers
description:
## Goal
Add `src/comment-markers.test.js` covering marker rendering and parsing edge cases.

## Coverage requirements
Test valid runner ID markers, valid session data markers, missing markers, empty body, malformed JSON, linked PR text such as `Changes in Pull Request #123`, linked PR Markdown URLs, and unknown legacy comment formats.

## Rationale
These markers are durable state, not cosmetic Markdown. A regression can cause duplicate agent runs or lost follow-up context.

## Success criteria
Tests fail clearly when marker format changes accidentally. Tests should use small inline comment samples and should not call GitHub or Netlify.
close_reason: Expanded comment marker helper tests to cover all required edge cases

title: Implement local simulator CLI
description:
## Goal
Add `src/simulate.js`, a local CLI that answers what the action would do for a fixture event without running GitHub Actions or calling Netlify.

## Required behavior
Support commands like `bun src/simulate.js --fixture fixtures/events/issue-comment-on-pr.json`, optional state fixture input, and `--format human|json|markdown`. The CLI should reuse `scenario-harness.js` and never duplicate scenario logic.

## Rationale
Maintainers need fast feedback while developing action behavior. The simulator turns fixture scenarios into an inspectable tool.

## Success criteria
The simulator prints should-run decision, context, model, prompt, recovered runner state, warnings, and would-render comments/summaries.
close_reason: Implemented local simulator CLI with state fixture support and focused tests

title: Refactor extract-agent-id to use state reconciliation
description:
## Goal
Update `src/extract-agent-id.js` so it fetches candidate bodies from GitHub APIs, delegates interpretation to `reconcileAgentState`, and sets existing outputs from the reconciled result.

## Required behavior
Keep current outputs: `agent-runner-id`, `session-data-map`, `agent-run-url`, `has-linked-pr`, and `linked-pr-number`. Add clear log warnings when state exists but is malformed. Do not change GitHub API permissions or fetch more data than needed.

## Rationale
The GitHub API wrapper should not also own parsing policy. Splitting fetch from reconciliation makes it easier to test and safer to evolve comment formats.

## Success criteria
Existing follow-up runs continue to resume the right runner. Deleted or malformed status comments do not throw. PR body fallback remains available.
close_reason: Refactored extract-agent-id to use state reconciliation with tests

title: Add tests for docs drift checker
description:
## Goal
Add `src/check-docs-drift.test.js` to validate the drift checker against both passing and intentionally broken samples.

## Coverage requirements
Detect wrong action slug, missing input documentation, missing output documentation, workflow template missing required permissions, and undeclared example inputs. Include an allowlist test if any field is intentionally undocumented.

## Rationale
The checker itself should not become another untested brittle parser.

## Success criteria
Tests are clear enough that maintainers can update docs and checker expectations together when public API changes.
close_reason: Expanded docs drift checker tests for missing input/output docs and workflow permissions drift

title: Triage and fix issues found during example-repo verification
description:
## Goal
Reserve explicit time to fix defects discovered only during live example-repo testing.

## Scope
Triage failures from the branch-ref smoke tests, map each to unit/scenario coverage where possible, patch the implementation, rerun local CI, and rerun the affected example-repo scenario.

## Rationale
Live GitHub Actions behavior can expose packaging, permissions, shell, and environment issues that fixture tests miss. This bead prevents treating the first live run as a ceremonial final check.

## Success criteria
Any live defect has a local regression test or documented reason why it cannot be simulated locally.
notes:
Live verification defect triage completed (2026-04-21):

Defect found:
- First live example-repo setup run failed before execution while loading `action.yml`.
- Failed run: https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example/actions/runs/24703482828
- Root cause: `CONTEXT_LABEL` used a GitHub expression containing `format('PR #{0}', ...)` without YAML-level quoting, so the `#` placeholder truncated the plain scalar and produced an unclosed expression during action manifest parsing.

Fix:
- Fixed in `dc8936170e8ecbd1704c95d49b5acfd756f5c08d` by YAML-quoting the `CONTEXT_LABEL` expression in `action.yml`.
- Added regression coverage in `src/action-wiring.test.js` to fail if any GitHub expression env value containing `#{...}` is left unquoted.

Verification:
- Focused regression: `bun test src/action-wiring.test.js` passed, 9 tests.
- Full suite: `bun test src/*.test.js` passed, 181 tests.
- Typecheck: `bunx tsc --noEmit` passed.
- Live reruns after the fix passed for preflight-only, dry-run, issue-created PR flow, and PR follow-up flow; invalid-site failure failed in the expected classified path.

No additional defects were found in the S4/S5 default-branch event verification.
close_reason: Triaged live verification defect and added regression coverage

title: Implement docs and template drift checker
description:
## Goal
Add `src/check-docs-drift.js` to mechanically validate that docs, examples, workflow templates, and `action.yml` stay aligned.

## Required checks
Parse declared inputs/outputs from `action.yml`; verify README input/output tables include public fields; verify examples use only declared inputs; verify canonical slug; verify workflow templates include required permissions and concurrency; verify important defaults such as model, dry-run, timeout, timezone, and any accepted `preflight-only` input.

## Rationale
Docs drift should fail in CI before users copy stale examples.

## Success criteria
The checker is deterministic, fast, and gives line/file-oriented error messages that explain what drift was found.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic docs/template drift checker, CI enforcement, and alignment fixes across public docs/examples

title: Release gate before moving @main or release tags
description:
## Goal
Create a final go/no-go gate before moving any public ref such as `@main` or a release tag after the hardening work lands.

## Required checks
Local `bun test src/*.test.js`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, docs drift checker, no bead dependency cycles, scenario harness pass, simulator sanity check, and successful example-repo smoke tests from `dw/actions-updates` or a commit SHA.

## Rationale
The user explicitly wants branch-based validation in the example repo before overwriting the working `@main` tag/ref. This gate codifies that release discipline.

## Success criteria
No public ref is moved until branch-based validation evidence is captured and reviewed.
notes:
Release gate status (2026-04-21):

Public refs:
- No action repo public ref was moved. `origin/main` and tags were not changed during verification.
- Example repo `master` was temporarily pinned for issue/comment event verification and then restored to `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@main` in `de8d8e2bad5357b50f61662b00bc76560ef4636f`.

Local checks completed:
- `bun test src/*.test.js` passed, 181 tests.
- `bunx tsc --noEmit` passed.
- `bun run docs:check` passed with `docs-drift: OK`.
- Simulator sanity passed: `bun run simulate -- --fixture fixtures/events/issue-opened-body-trigger.json --format json` returned `issues`, `run`, `true`, issue `10`.
- `br dep cycles --json` returned zero cycles.

Live verification completed:
- `agent-runner-action-jx3` is closed with live example-repo evidence for preflight-only, dry-run, normal issue-created PR flow, PR follow-up flow, and intentional invalid-site failure.
- `agent-runner-action-nbl` is closed with the live defect triaged and regression coverage added.

Residual note:
- `br doctor` exits 0 and reports DB/JSONL counts in sync plus write probe OK, but also reports SQLite integrity warnings for never-used pages in the local `.beads` DB. JSONL is synced; this does not block the code release gate, but the local DB should be rebuilt if the warnings matter operationally.

Hold this bead open until the explicit decision to move `@main` or any release tag.

title: Implement GitHub Actions step summary renderer
description:
## Goal
Add `src/generate-step-summary.js` to render a concise Markdown report for `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` on every triggered run.

## Required sections
Include run overview, trigger context, model, dry-run/preflight-only flags, agent details, dashboard/deploy/PR links, prompt, failure category/remediation when applicable, and preflight check results when applicable.

## Rationale
Maintainers often debug from the Actions run page. The summary should be the operator console that explains the run without reading raw logs.

## Success criteria
The renderer is pure, accepts data via an object or env-normalization wrapper, escapes Markdown table values, and does not call GitHub or Netlify APIs.
close_reason: Implemented pure step summary renderer and tests

title: Wire preflight mode and guards into action.yml
description:
## Goal
Add preflight execution to the composite action flow without breaking existing default behavior.

## Required behavior
Add `preflight-only` input if accepted. Run preflight after trigger/context extraction and before agent creation. Set outputs such as `preflight-ok`, `preflight-json`, `preflight-summary`, and `should-continue`. Gate downstream agent steps with `steps.preflight.outputs.should-continue == 'true'` where needed.

## UX requirements
For preflight-only success, explain that no agent was started. For preflight failure, show failed checks and remediation. Feed results into the step summary and status comment path where appropriate.

## Success criteria
Default runs still run normally. `preflight-only=true` validates setup and exits without creating or resuming an agent runner.
close_reason: Wired preflight-only flow into action.yml with gating, comments, outputs, and docs drift parity

title: Implement rich failure taxonomy module
description:
## Goal
Add `src/failure-taxonomy.js` to classify action failures into stable categories with user-facing titles, summaries, remediation steps, retryability, and user-action flags.

## Required categories
Cover missing auth token, missing site ID, site lookup failure, Netlify CLI missing/install failure, model unavailable, agent create failure, follow-up session create failure, agent timeout, agent failed, deploy preview unavailable, commit-to-branch failure, pull request creation failure, GitHub permission denied, GitHub API failure, malformed API response, and unknown.

## Rationale
Generic failure comments force users to inspect logs. Categorized failures let comments, step summaries, and future docs point users at the right fix.

## Success criteria
The classifier is pure, deterministic, and can be called from both error comment generation and step summary generation.
notes: Aligned failure-taxonomy module to bead input contract by handling state/errorMessage/exitCode/timeoutSeconds/model in detection path, and expanded src/failure-taxonomy.test.js coverage for those fields plus category detections. Verified with bun test src/failure-taxonomy.test.js and bun test src/*.test.js.
close_reason: Implemented failure taxonomy module and tests

title: Implement deterministic scenario harness
description:
## Goal
Add `src/scenario-harness.js` to execute action decision modules against fixture payloads without contacting GitHub or Netlify.

## Required behavior
Load event fixtures; create mock `github`, `context`, and `core`; run trigger/context/state/comment/failure/summary decisions; inject fake GitHub and Netlify responses; return a trace containing outputs, logs, comments, reconciled state, summary Markdown, warnings, and failures.

## Rationale
The action has many event branches. Unit tests for helpers are necessary but not enough to prove user workflows are intact.

## Success criteria
The harness is reusable by both scenario tests and the simulator CLI. It should make failures easy to diagnose by printing the scenario trace.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic scenario harness with fixture injection and scenario tests (4 pass)

title: Add unit tests for step summary rendering
description:
## Goal
Add `src/generate-step-summary.test.js` for summary rendering behavior.

## Coverage requirements
Success run includes outcome, context, model, runner ID, PR/deploy links; failure run includes failure category and remediation; timeout includes configured timeout; dry-run explains no PR/commit was created; preflight-only explains no agent was started; Markdown table escaping handles pipes and newlines.

## Rationale
The summary is user-facing diagnostics. Tests protect readability and correctness.

## Success criteria
Summary output is stable enough for scenario tests and useful enough for real debugging.
close_reason: Verified existing generate-step-summary test coverage satisfies all bead requirements; no code changes required

title: Normalize public docs and examples to canonical action slug
description:
## Goal
Ensure all public consumer examples use `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@v1` as the canonical action slug.

## Files to check
`README.md`, `docs/index.html`, `example-workflow.yml`, and `workflow-templates/netlify-agents.yml`.

## Rationale
The canonical slug is the one users should copy today. Drift between docs, templates, and examples creates broken setup paths and support noise.

## Success criteria
No public consumer example points at `netlify/agent-runner@v1`, `netlify/agent-runner-action@v1`, or another non-canonical slug unless an allowlist explicitly documents why.
close_reason: Normalized public examples to netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@v1 and added contract test coverage

title: Implement durable agent state reconciliation
description:
## Goal
Add `src/state-reconciliation.js` to reconstruct the best known Netlify Agent state from status comment body, PR body, linked PR hints, existing outputs, and site/runner metadata.

## Required behavior
Return a structured result with `runnerId`, `sessionDataMap`, `linkedPrNumber`, `agentRunUrl`, `confidence`, `sources`, `warnings`, and `recoveryAction`. Prefer valid status comment state over PR body fallback. Treat malformed session JSON as a warning, not a crash. Distinguish no state from broken state.

## Rationale
Follow-up prompts and issue-to-PR redirects depend on recovering state from comments that users or bots may edit or delete. A pure reconciliation layer lets the action recover consistently and gives scenario tests a stable assertion point.

## Success criteria
The module is pure and independently testable. It preserves current behavior for valid comments while adding safer fallback and warning behavior for malformed or partial state.
close_reason: Implemented pure state reconciliation module with tests

title: Define shared contracts for markers, failures, summaries, and fixtures
description:
## Goal
Establish the shared data contracts that later workstreams should rely on before implementation spreads more ad hoc strings and regexes across the codebase.

## Scope
Define the expected shapes for comment markers, reconciled state, failure classification, step-summary input, preflight check results, and scenario traces. This can be a design-only code task at first: JSDoc typedefs, module exports, and tests should make the contracts explicit.

## Rationale
The action currently has several implicit contracts embedded in comment bodies, shell outputs, and environment variables. Hardening depends on making these contracts concrete so modules can share one representation and tests can assert stable behavior.

## Success criteria
Future work can import shared helpers or typedefs instead of duplicating strings. The contracts preserve current behavior and support the example-repo branch verification flow.
notes: Added src/contracts.js shared contract module and src/contracts.test.js coverage for marker constants, failure categories/stages/severities, and factory contract defaults. Verified with bun test src/contracts.test.js and bun test src/*.test.js.

title: Add unit tests for failure taxonomy
description:
## Goal
Add `src/failure-taxonomy.test.js` covering all high-value failure categories and fallback behavior.

## Coverage requirements
Test missing token, missing site ID, model unavailable with alternate model suggestions, no agent ID from create, follow-up session retry exhaustion, timeout, PR create no URL, commit merge error, GitHub 403/404 permission denial, malformed JSON, and unknown fallback.

## Rationale
Failure classification becomes part of the public support contract. Tests should prevent accidental changes that make remediation less useful.

## Success criteria
Each category returns the expected `category`, useful title, remediation list, retryability, and user-action flag.
close_reason: Expanded failure taxonomy tests to cover all required high-value scenarios and actionable fields

title: Add preflight unit and scenario tests
description:
## Goal
Add `src/preflight.test.js` plus scenario coverage for preflight-only and preflight failure paths.

## Coverage requirements
Missing auth token, missing site ID, invalid model, invalid timeout, dry-run plus preflight-only warning, site lookup success, site lookup failure mapped to `site-lookup-failed`, preflight result in step summary, and no agent creation in preflight-only scenarios.

## Rationale
Preflight affects control flow and public UX. Tests must prove it validates setup without unexpectedly starting agents.

## Success criteria
Preflight behavior is deterministic and safe before live example-repo testing.
close_reason: Added missing preflight unit/scenario coverage for site-lookup success, preflight-only no-agent summary, and preflight failure summary

title: Wire step summary generation into action.yml
description:
## Goal
Add an `always()` step in `action.yml` that writes the generated Markdown report to `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` when the action was triggered.

## Required behavior
The step should no-op when `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` is unavailable. It should include success, failure, timeout, dry-run, and preflight-only state. It should use structured outputs from existing steps and failure taxonomy data where available.

## Rationale
The summary should exist even if comment update steps fail. It must not change normal action behavior or require additional permissions.

## Success criteria
Triggered runs produce a useful Actions summary. Skipped triggers do not spam summaries unless explicitly decided later.
close_reason: Wired always() step summary generation to GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY with preflight/failure context

title: Add tests for taxonomy-backed error comments
description:
## Goal
Add or expand `src/generate-error-comment.test.js` to verify the new taxonomy-backed error comment behavior.

## Coverage requirements
Test missing setup failures, model unavailable, timeout, GitHub permission failure, unknown fallback, link rendering, truncation/sanitization, and hidden marker preservation.

## Rationale
The status comment is durable user-facing UI. Tests should protect both content and hidden state markers.

## Success criteria
A future copy or formatting change cannot remove remediation guidance or hidden status markers unnoticed.
close_reason: Expanded taxonomy-backed error comment tests for setup/model/timeout/permission/unknown/link/sanitization/marker coverage.

title: Wire docs drift checker into CI
description:
## Goal
Update `package.json` and `.github/workflows/ci.yml` so docs drift checking runs with the normal validation path.

## Required behavior
Add a script such as `docs:check` that runs `bun src/check-docs-drift.js`. CI should run it after install and before or alongside tests/typecheck.

## Rationale
A checker that is not in CI will drift out of use. This should become part of the default quality gate.

## Success criteria
CI fails on docs/API drift and passes on the normalized docs state.
close_reason: Added docs:check script, wired CI docs drift gate, and fixed strict JS type errors; docs check/tests/typecheck pass

title: Prepare branch-based example-repo verification workflow
description:
## Goal
Prepare an explicit verification plan for `https://github.com/netlify-labs/agent-runner-action-example` that tests this action from the development branch before any `@main` or tag movement.

## Required approach
Use the action ref `netlify-labs/agent-runner-action@dw/actions-updates` or an immutable commit SHA from that branch in the example repo workflow. Do not overwrite or repoint the working `@main` tag/ref during validation. Capture which branch/SHA was tested.

## Scenarios to cover
Preflight-only success, invalid site ID failure, dry-run prompt, normal issue trigger, PR follow-up trigger, and a failure path with readable summary/comment output.

## Success criteria
There is a clear checklist for live validation in the example repo, including expected comments, summaries, links, and rollback behavior.
close_reason: Added branch-ref example-repo verification workflow/checklist with scenario coverage and contract test
